title: Funeral Hymn
content:
Dust unto dust,
    To this all must;
    The tenant hath resign'd
    The faded form To waste and worm
    Corruption claims her kind.

    Through paths unknown
    Thy soul hath flown,
    To seek the realms of woe,
    Where fiery pain
    Shall purge the stain
    Of actions done below.

    In that sad place,
    By Mary's grace,
    Brief may thy dwelling be
    Till prayers and alms,
    And holy psalms,
    Shall set the captive free.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter Scott (Sir), titled "Funeral Hymn", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
